_note: Synthetic mock data for educational and portfolio use only. No real patient information.

patients.raw_clinical_note: 67-year-old female with history of Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus, diagnosed 8 years ago. Currently on Metformin 500mg twice daily. Last HbA1c measured at 8.9%, above the target of 7.0%. Patient has not been seen in clinic for 45 days. eGFR last recorded at 75 mL/min/1.73m2, indicating normal renal function. No acute complaints reported. Patient reports occasional missed doses of medication.

54-year-old male with Hypertension, well-controlled on Lisinopril 10mg daily. Most recent visit was 12 days ago, blood pressure recorded at 128/82. eGFR 80 mL/min/1.73m2. No diabetes history, HbA1c not applicable. Patient reports good medication adherence and no new symptoms.

72-year-old female with Congestive Heart Failure (NYHA Class II) and Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus. Current medications include Furosemide 40mg daily and Metformin 1000mg twice daily. Most recent HbA1c is 9.2%, indicating poor glycemic control. eGFR measured at 25 mL/min/1.73m2, indicating stage 4 chronic kidney disease. Patient has not been seen in clinic for 89 days. No recent BNP or BMP on file.

expected_safety_flags: Metformin contraindicated — eGFR below 30, risk of lactic acidosis

patients.raw_clinical_note: 61-year-old male with Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus, managed with Insulin Glargine 20 units nightly. HbA1c recorded at 7.1%, near target. eGFR 68 mL/min/1.73m2, normal renal function. Last visit was 30 days ago. Patient reports stable blood glucose readings at home.

45-year-old female with mild persistent Asthma, using Albuterol inhaler as needed. No diabetes or hypertension history. eGFR 95 mL/min/1.73m2. Last visit was 10 days ago for routine follow-up. No acute exacerbations reported in the last 6 months.

80-year-old male with Chronic Kidney Disease Stage 4 and Atrial Fibrillation. Current medications include Digoxin 0.25mg daily and Lisinopril 10mg daily. eGFR measured at 22 mL/min/1.73m2. No HbA1c on file, patient is not diabetic. Patient has not been seen in clinic for 120 days. No recent digoxin level on file.

expected_safety_flags: Digoxin requires dose adjustment — eGFR below 30 increases risk of digoxin toxicity
Care gap of 120 days exceeds safe follow-up window for CKD Stage 4 patient
